summary: For where-like kernels, do not feed int8 condition tensors directly into AscendC CompareScalar. Cast the broadcasted boolean condition to float in python_bind_src and declare the op input as DT_FLOAT, otherwise CompareScalar may fail compilation because the source operand type is unsupported.

summary: A straightforward CompareScalar plus Select vector path can compile yet still trigger a vector-core exception for broadcasted where kernels. If this happens, treat the Select/mask pipeline as suspect rather than the op registration, and simplify the kernel compute path instead of only tweaking the host side.

summary: Reducing the mask buffer to count/8 bytes and inserting PipeBarrier calls was not sufficient to stabilize this where kernel. When vector Select keeps faulting after these low-level fixes, switch away from packed-mask vector selection and use a simpler tiled implementation.

A full-GM GetValue/SetValue loop produced incorrect all-zero output for this broadcast where op. The reliable fix was to copy small tiles into LocalTensor buffers, branch elementwise with GetValue/SetValue on the local tensors, and then copy the tile back to GM.
